title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. acutangula
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. acutangula, also known as the lady's mantle or alchemilla, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The medicinal properties of alchemilla include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce bleeding, and ease digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of alchemilla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from minor skin irritations to more serious conditions such as menstrual cramps and digestive disorders.
